title: ADR UI/API local y threat model de interfaz
wave: Ola 9 — API local y Web UI local web-ready

architectural_decision.reason: FUNC-SPRINT-64 es un gate arquitectónico: formaliza Web UI local como interfaz visual canónica de Fase F, FastAPI como API local futura, Web UI real como evolución posterior y Desktop diferido fuera de Fase F antes de implementar servidor o frontend.

summary.api_local_stack_decided: FastAPI planned as optional API dependency in a later sprint
summary.frontend_direction_decided: ui/web TypeScript/Vite, React allowed when UI sprint implements components

pass_criteria: ADR ratifies Web UI local as Phase F canonical visual interface
ADR documents Web UI real as future evolution and Desktop as deferred
Threat model covers localhost, CORS, token, CSRF/local origin, secrets, path traversal, reports/traces and critical actions
Application contract remains ok=true and reports web-first strategy
No server, frontend or desktop shell is implemented in Sprint 64
block_criteria: Desktop is reopened as Phase F implementation scope without later ADR
Threat model is missing or omits CORS/token/secrets/actions
CORS wildcard is allowed by default
Server or frontend implementation is added before the ADR/threat model gate

risks.mitigation: Threat model requires token, origin/CORS constraints and policy binding in later sprints.
ADR and contract require UI -> API -> ApplicationService.
Desktop is deferred outside Fase F and requires later ADR.
Sprint 64 does not add dependencies; Sprint 67 must use optional API extra or documented dependency boundary.
